summary: Fair soundboard recording with a standout 30‑minute Dark Star and strong Keith piano work despite tape degradation.
review: The show shines with an epic Dark Star that stretches nearly half an hour, backed by hot versions of Good Lovin', Caution and a fine piano performance from Keith. Recording quality is only average – a degraded SBD tape that sounds subdued and even needs a slight speed correction – but the musical payoff makes it worth the listen for Deadheads.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviewers consistently rate the set at 3 stars, praising the performance (especially Dark Star) while noting poor to fair sound quality and technical issues.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Degraded SBD tape gives a B‑ grade sound; subdued mids, some EQ needed, and a reported 2.8–2.85% speed issue despite pitch correction.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A long, exploratory jam heavy second set with Dark Star flowing into Sugar Magnolia and a sizzling Caution; overall the band plays off each other with strong improvisational energy.

band_member_comments.Keys: Keith's piano work is highlighted as fine and musically satisfying.
